sub_tasks.requirements: Method and code fidelity to the paper

train.py (or equivalent) implements the SDAR token-level gap gate g_t = σ(β · Δ_t) where Δ_t = log π_teacher(y_t|·) − log π_student(y_t|·) is the detached single-sample reverse-KL-aligned Teacher-Student log-probability gap on the student-sampled token, as defined in Section 2.3 and Appendix A.
sub_tasks.task_category: Method and code fidelity to the paper

sub_tasks.requirements: The overall training objective combines GRPO policy loss with the SDAR auxiliary loss as L = L_GRPO + λ · L_OPSD with λ=0.1 and sigmoid sharpness β=10 (Section 3.1 'we set λ=0.1 and β=10' and Table 3).
sub_tasks.task_category: Method and code fidelity to the paper

sub_tasks.requirements: All five baselines are re-implemented under the same Qwen backbones: GRPO (Algorithm 2), OPSD (Algorithm 3), Skill-SD with importance-weighted f-divergence + KM skill retrieval (Algorithm 4), GRPO+OPSD additive auxiliary (Algorithm 5), and RLSD which re-weights GRPO advantages by the self-teacher gap (Algorithm 6), as specified in Appendix A.
sub_tasks.task_category: Method and code fidelity to the paper

sub_tasks.requirements: Three gating strategies from Section 2.3 are selectable in code: Entropy gate g_t=σ(β·H_t), Gap gate g_t=σ(β·Δ_t), and Soft-OR gate g_t=σ(β·H_t)+σ(β·Δ_t)−σ(β·H_t)·σ(β·Δ_t), with gap gating as the default per Section 3.3.
sub_tasks.task_category: Method and code fidelity to the paper

sub_tasks.requirements: Data and preprocessing fidelity

ALFWorld environment is set up with the 3,827 task instances spanning the six categories Pick, Look, Clean, Heat, Cool, Pick2, using the training data split from GiGPO (Feng et al. 2025), as described in Section 3.

Search-QA setup follows Search-R1 (Jin et al. 2025) using E5 (Wang et al. 2022) as the retriever, with NQ + HotpotQA as in-domain training data and TriviaQA, PopQA, 2Wiki, MuSiQue, Bamboogle as out-of-domain evaluation, per Section 3.
WebShop training uses 1000 selected training tasks and evaluation uses the 128 fixed validation tasks aligned with Feng et al. 2025, as stated in Section 3.

Skill retrieval uses the SkillBank from SkillRL (Xia et al. 2026) across all three environments and implements the four retrieval strategies: UCB (Equation 1), Keyword Matching (KM), Full Retrieval, and Random Retrieval, per Section 2.1 and Section 3.
Experiment execution and reproducibility
Training is executed for 150 steps on the Qwen2.5-Instruct and Qwen3-Instruct families (Qwen2.5-3B-Instruct, Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct, Qwen3-1.7B-Instruct) as specified in Section 3.
ALFWorld training uses batch of 16 tasks × 8 rollouts per prompt with max prompt length 2,048 tokens (Section 3).
Search-QA training uses batch of 128 tasks with max prompt length 4,096 tokens (Section 3).
WebShop training uses batch of 16 tasks × 8 rollouts per prompt with max prompt length 4,096 tokens (Section 3).

Run scripts target the 8× H800 GPU hardware spec from Section 3 (or a documented equivalent), with seeds and full command line recorded so the SDAR runs for each (model, environment) pair are reproducible.
Evaluation protocol and metric correctness

Inference-time evaluation of SDAR uses prompts with the {skill_context} field LEFT EMPTY (no skills at test time), matching the design that SDAR 'requires no external skills during inference' in Section 3.2 and Figures 15–17.
Diagnostic metrics during training include the Teacher-Student gap mean Δ̄, gate activation ratio (fraction with g_t > 0.5), gate mean, OPSD loss, and reward curve, as plotted in Figures 5 and 10–14.
Result match versus the paper's reported targets
SDAR on Qwen2.5-3B achieves ALFWorld success rate ≈ 84.4% (vs. GRPO 75.0%, a +9.4% gain) as reported in Section 3.2 and Table 1.
sub_tasks.task_category: Result match versus the paper's reported targets

sub_tasks.requirements: SDAR on Qwen2.5-7B achieves the reported +7.0% Search-QA and +10.2% WebShop-Acc improvements over GRPO, per the Abstract and Section 3.2.
sub_tasks.task_category: Result match versus the paper's reported targets

sub_tasks.requirements: SDAR on Qwen3-1.7B reaches the highest reported ALFWorld score of 53.9% (vs. GRPO 46.1%, Skill-GRPO 21.1%, RLSD 42.2%), per Section 3.2 and Table 1.
sub_tasks.task_category: Result match versus the paper's reported targets

sub_tasks.requirements: Reproduced naive GRPO+OPSD exhibits the documented catastrophic instability — degrading to ~32.0 on Qwen3-1.7B ALFWorld vs. GRPO's 46.1, and standalone OPSD collapses to near-zero on Search-QA — as described in Section 3.2 and Table 1.
sub_tasks.task_category: Result match versus the paper's reported targets

sub_tasks.requirements: Ablation: λ=0.1 with β=10 outperforms λ=1.0 and λ=0.01, and β=10 outperforms β∈{1,5,50,∞} (no-gate uniform distillation), reproducing the trends in Figures 7–9 of Section 3.3.
sub_tasks.task_category: Result match versus the paper's reported targets

sub_tasks.requirements: Robustness: across the four retrieval tiers (UCB, KM, Full, Random) all four outperform the pure GRPO baseline on ALFWorld / WebShop-Score / WebShop-Acc, matching Table 2 in Section 3.2.
sub_tasks.task_category: Result match versus the paper's reported targets

sub_tasks.requirements: Artifact completeness and provenance

Repository contains the SDAR Algorithm 1 implementation plus the five baseline algorithms (GRPO, OPSD, Skill-SD, GRPO+OPSD, RLSD) corresponding to Algorithms 2–6 in Appendix A, with code organized so each is runnable.

The ALFWorld, Search-QA, and WebShop prompt templates from Figures 15–17 are included verbatim with the {skill_context} placeholder, supporting both training-time skill injection and inference-time empty-skill runs.
Hyperparameter configuration files reproduce Table 3 (η, G, ε, λ=0.1, β=10, α_KL, SRS=KM) for each method × environment combination.

A final_report / metrics artifact is produced reporting numbers in the same Table 1 layout (ALFWorld success %, Search-QA accuracy %, WebShop Score/Acc %), with provenance pointing to the public code at https://github.com/ZJU-REAL/SDAR.
